name: Abu Saleh Mohammad Rafi, PhD
title: Associate Professor
bio_raw:
Abu Saleh Mohammad Rafi
, PhD, is an Associate Professor of Applied Linguistics at the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh. He holds a Visiting Research Fellowship at the University of Groningen, Netherlands, and is a Senior Research Fellow at the University of South Australia, contributing to the Australian Research Council–funded national project Starting Young: Early Years Language Learning in Australia.
Dr Rafi’s research focuses on multilingualism, translanguaging pedagogies, language policy, and critical AI studies. He has published extensively in leading international journals in applied linguistics, including Applied Linguistics (Oxford University Press), English Today (Cambridge University Press), ELT Journal (Oxford University Press), Applied Linguistics Review, International Journal of Educational Development, International Journal of Multilingualism, Language, Identity & Education, Teaching in Higher Education, Classroom Discourse, and Critical Inquiry in Language Studies. His work also appears in invited chapters in edited volumes published by Springer, Routledge, and Vernon Press, and he co-edited the 2023 Critical Inquiry in Language Studies special issue.
In 2024, Dr Rafi convened the International Conference on the Trans‑Phenomenon in Language, Literature, and Culture, hosted by the Department of English and Humanities at the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh. Later that year, he was recognized as the Rising Scholar at Language Metamorphosis: Implications for Language Education in Decolonial Contexts (LMLEDC), organized by the BRAC Institute of Languages (BIL) at BRAC University, Bangladesh (28–30 November 2024). In 2025, he was invited by the Bangladesh University Grants Commission (UGC) to serve as Project Evaluator for the national Higher Education Acceleration and Transformation (HEAT) Project (20–22 May). Subsequently, he contributed to the International Postgraduate & Early-Career Researchers in Applied Linguistics Symposium (IPEALS 2025) as an Invited Mentor and Discussant, supporting emerging voices in applied linguistics in a virtual symposium organized by the Malaysian Association of Applied Linguistics (21 December 2025). He was also an Invited Panel Speaker at the International Conference on Challenges and Trends in Arts and Social Sciences (ICCTASS 2025), hosted by the American International University–Bangladesh (12 December 2025), where he shared insights on AI ethics, education, and legal frameworks for building a sustainable future.
Dr Rafi has previously delivered keynote addresses at the 10th and 11th International Conferences on Language and Literacy Education at the University of the Witwatersrand, Johannesburg, South Africa, and at the International Conference on Translation and Intercultural Education (Wuhan Forum) at China Three Gorges University. He has given invited talks at institutions including McGill University (Canada), James Cook University (Australia), University of Groningen (Netherlands), UMT University (Pakistan), Buriram Rajabhat University (Thailand), and over fifteen other universities across the Global South and North. He offered a session at the 2025 DAAD Winter School and delivered a keynote at the Online International Conference on Second Language Writing at Tlemcen University, Algeria, on 11–12 February 2026.
stated_interests: Translanguaging, Critical AI Studies, Applied Linguistics, Educational Linguistics, Sociolinguistics, Critical Race Theory, Multicultural Education

Education
Doctor of Philosophy (Linguistics)
James Cook University, Australia | 2018–2022
Thesis:
Pedagogical Benefits, Ideological and Practical Challenges, and Implementational Spaces of a Translanguaging Education Policy: The Case of Bangladeshi Higher Education
Supervisors:
Anne-Marie Morgan; Florence Boulard; Finex Ndhlovu; Susan Feez.
Examination outcome: Exceptional (Top 5%).
Master of Arts (Sociolinguistics)
Liverpool Hope University, United Kingdom | 2015–2017
First in overall merit order.
